trips.name: Provence Lavender & Luberon
trips.description: In July, the Valensole plateau turns violet with lavender as far as the eye can see. Drive through ochre-cliffed Roussillon, perched Gordes, and the Luberon's hilltop villages for the quintessential Provence experience.
trips.cover_search_query: Provence lavender fields Valensole France

trips.name: Côte d'Azur Classic
trips.description: The French Riviera's Corniche roads — Grande, Moyenne, and Basse — offer three different perspectives on Monaco and the cliff-backed coastline between Nice and Menton. Glamour without flying.
trips.cover_search_query: Côte d'Azur Corniche road Nice France

trips.name: Gorges du Verdon — Europe's Grand Canyon
trips.description: The Gorges du Verdon is Europe's deepest canyon — 700m of limestone walls above a turquoise river. The D952 road along the rim is one of France's great drives, with vertigo-inducing viewpoints.
highlights: Canoe and kayak on the lake
trips.cover_search_query: Gorges du Verdon canyon turquoise France

trips.name: Alpilles, Arles & the Camargue
trips.description: Les Baux-de-Provence perches above olive-silvered limestone while the Camargue wetlands shelter white horses and flamingos. Van Gogh painted Arles relentlessly; the Roman arena still hosts corridas.
trips.cover_search_query: Camargue white horses Provence France

trips.name: Aix-en-Provence & Marseille
trips.description: Cézanne's fountained city and France's oldest harbour: Aix's cours Mirabeau and Marseille's Vieux-Port are just 30 minutes apart but feel like different worlds. The Calanques between them are extraordinary.
trips.cover_search_query: Calanques limestone cliffs Marseille France

trips.name: Var — Saint-Tropez & the Massif des Maures
trips.description: Saint-Tropez's harbour bristles with superyachts, but inland the Massif des Maures is a wild cork-oak forest hiding villages like Collobrières and its marron glacé tradition.
trips.cover_search_query: Saint-Tropez harbour yachts Var France

trips.name: Haute-Provence — Lavender & Mountains
trips.description: Above Digne-les-Bains, the Alpes-de-Haute-Provence open into a landscape of lavender fields, alpine villages, and the remote Vallée de l'Ubaye — all within a long day's drive of the Côte d'Azur.
trips.cover_search_query: Sisteron citadel rock Haute-Provence France

trips.name: D-Day Beaches & the Battle of Normandy
trips.description: The 80km of Normandy coast between Utah and Sword beaches is the most significant stretch of 20th-century history. Walk Omaha Beach, visit Pointe du Hoc, and end at the moving American cemetery above the sea.
trips.cover_search_query: Omaha Beach Normandy D-Day cemetery France

trips.name: Mont Saint-Michel & the Cotentin
trips.description: Mont Saint-Michel rises from tidal sands like a medieval hallucination — the most visited site in France outside Paris. The Cotentin peninsula's wild cliffs and Cap de la Hague complete this corner of Normandy.
trips.cover_search_query: Mont Saint-Michel tidal island Normandy France

trips.name: Honfleur, Étretat & the Alabaster Coast
trips.description: Honfleur's Vieux Bassin inspired Boudin and later Monet. North on the Alabaster Coast, Étretat's chalk arches plunge into the sea in the scene that inspired Monet's most famous canvases.
trips.cover_search_query: Étretat chalk cliffs arch Normandy France

trips.name: Norman Switzerland & the Perche
trips.description: The wooded valleys of the Suisse Normande earned their name from the dramatic scenery. The Perche countryside beyond produces its famous percherons horses and France's finest cider and calvados.
trips.cover_search_query: Suisse Normande river gorge Normandy France

trips.name: Finistère — Land's End of Brittany
trips.description: Finistère means Land's End in Latin — it's where France runs out, and the Atlantic takes over. Crozon's wild peninsulas, Ouessant's lighthouse island, and the enclos paroissiaux carved churchyards are unforgettable.
trips.cover_search_query: Pointe du Raz Finistère Brittany France

trips.name: Saint-Malo, Dinard & the Emerald Coast
trips.description: Saint-Malo's ramparts enclose a corsair city that seems lifted from a novel. Across the estuary Dinard has striped beach tents and villas; east, the Côte d'Émeraude's green water matches its name.
trips.cover_search_query: Saint-Malo ramparts Brittany France

trips.name: Gulf of Morbihan & Carnac
trips.description: The Gulf of Morbihan is a sheltered inland sea full of islands and oyster beds. Carnac's stone alignments — 3,000 ancient menhirs stretching for kilometres — are prehistoric Europe's greatest mystery.
trips.cover_search_query: Carnac megalithic stones Brittany France

trips.name: Pink Granite Coast & the Tregor
trips.description: The Côte de Granit Rose turns pink at sunrise, its enormous rounded boulders creating a lunar landscape at Ploumanac'h. Perros-Guirec and Tréguier's Gothic cathedral anchor this northern Breton circuit.
trips.cover_search_query: Pink Granite Coast Ploumanac'h Brittany France

trips.name: Loire Valley Châteaux
trips.description: The Loire valley is the garden of France — a UNESCO river corridor of Renaissance châteaux, white tuffeau stone villages, and cool white wines. Chambord, Chenonceau, and Amboise define French royal grandeur.
trips.cover_search_query: Château de Chambord Loire Valley France

trips.name: Anjou & the Western Loire
trips.description: The western Loire is wilder and less visited — Angers's tapestry of the Apocalypse, the muscadet vineyards of the Pays Nantais, and the extraordinary troglodyte village of Rochemenier.
trips.cover_search_query: Saumur castle Loire Valley France

trips.name: Alsace Wine Route
trips.description: The Route des Vins d'Alsace threads through half-timbered villages with flower-draped balconies, producing Riesling, Gewürztraminer, and Pinot Gris under a Vosges mountain backdrop.
trips.cover_search_query: Alsace Riquewihr half-timbered village wine France

trips.name: Vosges Mountains & the Alsatian Crest Road
trips.description: The Route des Crêtes runs along the crest of the Vosges from one WWII battlefield to the next, with views across to the Black Forest. Munster valley produces the eponymous pungent cheese.
trips.cover_search_query: Vosges mountains Route des Crêtes Alsace France

trips.name: Lorraine — Metz, Nancy & the Moselle
trips.description: Nancy's Place Stanislas is the most beautiful baroque square in France; Metz has a cathedral of stained glass; and the Moselle river curves between vineyards producing the lightest wines in France.
trips.cover_search_query: Nancy Place Stanislas baroque square France

trips.name: Burgundy Wine Road — Côte d'Or
trips.description: The Route des Grands Crus runs through 35km of the world's most valuable farmland, passing the appellations of Gevrey-Chambertin, Vosne-Romanée, and Meursault. Every village here is a wine legend.
trips.cover_search_query: Burgundy Côte d'Or vineyards Beaune France

trips.name: Vézelay, Auxerre & Northern Burgundy
trips.description: Vézelay's basilica on a hilltop was the mustering point for the Second and Third Crusades. Northern Burgundy's Chablis vineyards produce the driest Chardonnay in France from ancient Kimmeridgian limestone.
trips.cover_search_query: Vézelay hilltop basilica Burgundy France

trips.name: Dordogne & Périgord Noir
trips.description: The Dordogne valley is prehistoric France — Lascaux cave paintings, walnut-tree-lined rivers, and foie gras country. Sarlat's golden medieval centre is the best-preserved bastide town in the southwest.
trips.cover_search_query: La Roque-Gageac Dordogne river cliffs France

trips.name: Lot Valley & Quercy Blanc
trips.description: The Lot river carves a dramatic incised meander through Cahors wine country and the Quercy Blanc. Figeac, Saint-Cirq-Lapopie, and Puy-l'Évêque perch above the river in positions of seemingly impossible beauty.
trips.cover_search_query: Saint-Cirq-Lapopie cliff village Lot France

trips.name: Bastide Towns of the Périgord
trips.description: The Périgord was divided between English and French in the Hundred Years War and its bastide towns — planned medieval new towns — still carry the grid layout and cornières arcades of the 13th century.
trips.cover_search_query: Monpazier bastide town Dordogne France

trips.name: Basque Country & the Atlantic Pyrenees
trips.description: The Basque coast from Bayonne to the Spanish border is Europe's surfing capital. Behind Biarritz, the Pyrenean foothills hide the pilgrimage village of Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port and the gorge of Kakueta.
trips.cover_search_query: Biarritz surf coast Basque France

trips.name: Hautes-Pyrénées & the Cirque de Gavarnie
trips.description: The Cirque de Gavarnie is a UNESCO natural amphitheatre with Europe's highest waterfall plunging 422m from a glacial cirque. Lourdes and Cauterets thermal spa valley complete this magnificent mountain circuit.
trips.cover_search_query: Cirque de Gavarnie Pyrenees waterfall France

trips.name: Cathar Country — Carcassonne & the Corbières
trips.description: The Cathar heresy left a trail of ruined mountain fortresses across the Corbières hills. Carcassonne's perfectly preserved double-walled cité is the largest medieval fortress in Europe.
trips.cover_search_query: Carcassonne medieval fortress Languedoc France

trips.name: Roman Languedoc — Nîmes & the Pont du Gard
trips.description: Languedoc preserves more intact Roman monuments than Rome itself. The Pont du Gard aqueduct, Nîmes's amphitheatre, and the Via Domitia road between them form the world's greatest Roman open-air museum.
trips.cover_search_query: Pont du Gard Roman aqueduct Languedoc France

trips.name: Roussillon — Catalan France
trips.description: Roussillon is France that speaks Catalan — red-roofed Perpignan's palace of the Kings of Mallorca, vineyards producing Banyuls and Collioure, and the Vermilion Coast's painted-scene fishing villages.
trips.cover_search_query: Collioure harbour Vermilion Coast Roussillon France

trips.name: Chamonix Valley & Mont Blanc
trips.description: The highest peak in the Alps towers 4,808m above Chamonix, accessible by the Aiguille du Midi cable car. The Chamonix valley connects six ski resorts and one of the world's great mountain drives.
trips.cover_search_query: Chamonix Mont Blanc glacier Alps France

trips.name: Annecy, Aravis & the Savoie Lakes
trips.description: Annecy sits at the edge of Europe's clearest lake, its old town of canals and pastel houses backdropped by limestone Aravis peaks. The Savoie region beyond serves tartiflette and raclette with Apremont wine.
trips.cover_search_query: Annecy old town canal Alps France

trips.name: Écrins & Vercors Massif
trips.description: The Vercors is a high limestone plateau with sheer cliffs on all sides — a Resistance stronghold in WWII. To the east, the Écrins national park has the most glaciated mountains in the French Alps.
trips.cover_search_query: Vercors plateau cliffs Grenoble France

trips.name: Bordeaux & Saint-Émilion
trips.description: Bordeaux's waterfront Cité du Vin celebrates the wine that made this city its fortune. Saint-Émilion's village of golden limestone sits atop limestone caves full of Merlot grands crus.
trips.cover_search_query: Saint-Émilion wine village Bordeaux France

trips.name: Médoc — Châteaux of the Left Bank
trips.description: The D2 through the Médoc is the most prestigious wine road in the world — Margaux, Pauillac, Saint-Estèphe — where classified estates produce the world's most valuable red wines among gentle gravel-covered hills.
trips.cover_search_query: Médoc wine château Pauillac Bordeaux France

trips.name: Arcachon Bay & the Landes Forests
trips.description: The Dune du Pilat is Europe's highest sand dune, rising 106m above the Atlantic. Arcachon Bay's oyster beds and the endless pine forests of the Landes extend south to the Basque country.
highlights: Dune du Pilat Europe's highest dune
trips.cover_search_query: Dune du Pilat sand dune Atlantic France

trips.name: Champagne Country — Reims & Épernay
trips.description: The Montagne de Reims and Vallée de la Marne are the two arms of Champagne country. Every great house has cellars beneath Épernay's Avenue de Champagne; Reims cathedral is where French kings were crowned.
trips.cover_search_query: Épernay Champagne Avenue cellars France

trips.name: Auvergne Volcanoes & Puy de Dôme
trips.description: The Chaîne des Puys — a UNESCO line of 80 volcanoes — is France's greatest geological wonder. Climb Puy de Dôme by rack railway for views over the crater chain, then descend to the thermal spas of Vichy.
trips.cover_search_query: Puy de Dôme volcano chain Auvergne France

trips.name: Gorges du Tarn & Millau Viaduct
trips.description: The Tarn slices 500m deep through the Causses limestone plateau in one of France's most spectacular gorges. The Millau Viaduct — the world's tallest bridge — crosses the valley at a height above the clouds.
trips.cover_search_query: Millau Viaduct bridge Gorges du Tarn France

trips.name: Corsica — Bonifacio & the South
trips.description: Corsica's southern tip is its most dramatic — Bonifacio's citadel hangs over white limestone cliffs above the Strait. Porto-Vecchio's gulf is ringed by the finest white beaches in the Mediterranean.
trips.cover_search_query: Bonifacio cliff citadel Corsica France

trips.name: Corsica — Cap Corse & the North
trips.description: Cap Corse is a narrow spine of mountains jutting into the Ligurian Sea, ringed with Genovese watch towers and tiny fishing ports. Calvi's citadel and the Balagne's olive-terraced hills extend westward.
trips.cover_search_query: Cap Corse coastline Corsica France

trips.name: Rhône Valley — Côtes du Rhône Wine Road
trips.description: The Rhône narrows between Lyon and Avignon to create France's most varied wine corridor — from the granite-terraced Syrah of Hermitage in the north to the Grenache blends of Châteauneuf-du-Pape.
trips.cover_search_query: Rhône Valley vineyards Hermitage France

trips.name: Lyon & the Beaujolais Villages
trips.description: Lyon is France's second gastronomy capital — its bouchon restaurants serve quenelles, cervelles de canut, and andouillette. The Beaujolais hills north produce the famous nouveau and ten distinguished cru villages.
trips.cover_search_query: Beaujolais vineyards village Lyon France

trips.name: Pays d'Auge — Cheese & Cider Country
trips.description: The Pays d'Auge's half-timbered manor farms produce Camembert, Livarot, Pont-l'Évêque, and the three great Norman drinks — cidre, calvados, and pommeau. This is rural France at its most delicious.
trips.cover_search_query: Beuvron-en-Auge half-timbered village Normandy France

trips.name: Paris & the Île-de-France Châteaux
trips.description: Paris cannot be summarised. But the drive out through the forests of the Île-de-France leads to Versailles, Fontainebleau, Vaux-le-Vicomte, and Chartres — a circuit of French royal grandeur.
trips.cover_search_query: Palace of Versailles gardens Île-de-France France

trips.name: Opal Coast & Flemish France
trips.description: The Opal Coast from Calais to the Somme mouth has white cliffs, beach huts, and herring ports. Inland, Flemish France — Lille's Grand Place, Arras's Baroque squares, and WWI Vimy Ridge — is rarely visited.
trips.cover_search_query: Cap Blanc-Nez white cliffs Opal Coast France

trips.name: Alsace Christmas Markets Circuit
trips.description: Alsace invented the Christmas tree tradition. In December every village transforms — Strasbourg's Christkindelsmärik, Colmar's old town glowing with stalls, and Ribeauvillé's medieval walls strung with lights.
highlights: Strasbourg Christmas market oldest in France
trips.cover_search_query: Strasbourg Christmas market Alsace France

trips.name: Normandy Cider Route
trips.description: The Cider Route loops through the apple orchards and stone farmyards of the Pays d'Auge, linking farm cideries and distilleries where calvados ages in oak barrels.
trips.cover_search_query: Normandy apple orchard cidre farm France

trips.name: Morvan Regional Park
trips.description: The Morvan is Burgundy's wild granite heart — a national park of oak forests, reservoirs, and hilltop villages that produced the chauffeurs of Paris in the 19th century and Resistance fighters in the 20th.
trips.cover_search_query: Semur-en-Auxois pink granite village Burgundy France

trips.name: Loire Troglodytes — The Cave Dwellers
trips.description: The soft tuffeau limestone of the Loire valley has been carved into cave dwellings, wine cellars, and even entire villages for thousands of years. Rochemenier and Troo are the most remarkable.
trips.cover_search_query: Troo troglodyte cave village Loire France

trips.name: Jura — Yellow Wine & Comté Cheese
trips.description: The Jura is France's best-kept wine secret — Vin Jaune ages under a veil of yeast for six years to produce something between sherry and Burgundy. The Comté cheese caves of Poligny are a pilgrimage for food lovers.
trips.cover_search_query: Château-Chalon Jura vineyards France

trips.name: Ariège & the Pyrenean Caves
trips.description: The Ariège has more prehistoric decorated caves than anywhere on earth — Niaux, Mas d'Azil, and Bédeilhac preserve Magdalenian art 13,000 years old. The medieval Cathar fortress of Montségur crowns the route.
trips.cover_search_query: Montségur Cathar fortress Ariège France

trips.name: Colmar & the Alsatian Villages
trips.description: Colmar is the most perfectly preserved medieval Alsatian city — its Little Venice quarter of canals and flowers is as pretty as anything in Europe. The surrounding villages of Eguisheim and Hunawihr are equally beautiful.
trips.cover_search_query: Colmar Little Venice canal Alsace France

trips.name: Belle-Île & the Morbihan Islands
trips.description: Belle-Île is the largest island off the Breton coast — Monet painted its dramatic stacks and grottoes in the 1880s. The ferry from Quiberon takes 45 minutes to reach a car-free Atlantic world of heathland and coves.
trips.cover_search_query: Belle-Île-en-Mer rocky coast Brittany France

trips.name: Margeride & Haute-Loire Plateaux
trips.description: The granite plateau of the Margeride is the wildest heartland of the Massif Central — a landscape of ancient beech forests, megalithic dolmens, and the Lozère wolf country that inspired the Beast of Gévaudan legend.
trips.cover_search_query: Le Puy-en-Velay volcanic rock cathedral France

trips.name: Cévennes — Stevenson Trail
trips.description: Robert Louis Stevenson walked the Cévennes with a donkey in 1878 and described a wilderness that hasn't changed much since — chestnut forests, Protestant villages, and the Gorges du Gardon descending to Pont du Gard.
trips.cover_search_query: Cévennes chestnut forest Massif Central France

trips.name: Alsace Castles & the Haut-Koenigsbourg
trips.description: The Vosges foothills above the Alsace plain are defended by a chain of medieval castles. The Haut-Koenigsbourg's restored fortress dominates the plain; Ribeauvillé below has three ruined towers on the ridge above it.
trips.cover_search_query: Haut-Koenigsbourg castle Alsace France

trips.name: Lot-et-Garonne — Plum Country
trips.description: Lot-et-Garonne is France's market garden — the pruneaux d'Agen come from here, as do the strawberries of Marmande. Agen and Villeneuve-sur-Lot flank a bastide circuit through sunflower fields.
trips.cover_search_query: Pujols hilltop village Lot-et-Garonne France

trips.name: Hérault — Pézenas, Montpellier & the Etang
trips.description: Pézenas was the Versailles of Languedoc — where Molière performed his early comedies in a city of hôtels particuliers. The Bassin de Thau's oyster tables and the Canal du Midi complete an elegant southern circuit.
trips.cover_search_query: Sète lagoon fishermen Languedoc France

trips.name: Charente & the Cognac Country
trips.description: The Charente river flows through cognac country — limestone cellars of aging brandy, Romanesque churches on the pilgrimage route, and the oyster ports of the Île d'Oléron.
trips.cover_search_query: Cognac distillery Charente France

trips.name: Marais Poitevin — Green Venice
trips.description: The Marais Poitevin is a labyrinth of waterways through poplar-canopied flatbottom boat country, earned the name Green Venice for its flat-bottomed barque tradition. Niort and Fontenay-le-Comte anchor either end.
trips.cover_search_query: Marais Poitevin Green Venice canals France

trips.name: Provençal Garrigue & the Pont du Gard
trips.description: The garrigue is the wild aromatic scrubland of Provence — thyme, lavender, and rosemary buzzing with cicadas. Between Avignon and Nîmes, the Pont du Gard emerges above a wooded gorge unchanged since Roman times.
trips.cover_search_query: Avignon Papal Palace Provence France

trips.name: Savoie & the French Shore of Lake Geneva
trips.description: The Chablais vineyards slope down to France's Lac Léman shore between Évian and Geneva. Thonon, Yvoire's flower village, and the Chablais mountains behind make a perfect loop between two countries.
trips.cover_search_query: Yvoire medieval village Lake Geneva France

trips.name: Sologne — The Secret Loire Hinterland
trips.description: The Sologne is a land of lakes, forests, and hunting châteaux hidden south of the Loire valley proper. Romorantin, the asparagus fields, and the white horses of the Camargue-style marshes make this a delightful back road.
trips.cover_search_query: Sologne lake forest Loire France

trips.name: Haute-Garonne & the Comminges
trips.description: The Comminges is a deeply rural Pyrenean hinterland sheltering a Roman city at Saint-Bertrand and the pilgrimage villages of Lourdes county. Bagnères-de-Luchon is the queen of Pyrenean spa towns.
trips.cover_search_query: Saint-Bertrand-de-Comminges cathedral Pyrenees France

trips.name: Norman Abbey Route — Benedictine Legacy
trips.description: William the Conqueror's duchy was built on Benedictine abbeys — Caen's Abbaye aux Hommes, Jumièges's romantic ruins, and Fécamp's abbatiale are testament to one of medieval Europe's greatest building programmes.
highlights: William the Conqueror Abbaye aux Hommes
trips.cover_search_query: Jumièges abbey ruins Normandy France

trips.name: Cluny & the Mâconnais Wine Hills
trips.description: Cluny was the most powerful abbey in medieval Christendom — at one point larger than St Peter's in Rome. The Mâconnais hills above the Saône produce Pouilly-Fuissé white wine and one of Burgundy's most beautiful landscapes.
trips.cover_search_query: Cluny abbey Burgundy France

trips.name: Cézanne's Provence — Sainte-Victoire
trips.description: Cézanne painted the limestone ridge of Sainte-Victoire more than 80 times from his studio in Aix. The walking trails above the plain of the Arc provide the same view that transfixed the Post-Impressionist master.
trips.cover_search_query: Cézanne Sainte-Victoire mountain Aix Provence France

trips.name: Troyes & the Champagne Lakes
trips.description: Troyes is a half-timbered city shaped like a Champagne cork, its medieval streets still largely intact. East of the city, the man-made Lac du Der is Europe's largest reservoir and a mecca for wintering cranes.
trips.cover_search_query: Troyes half-timbered medieval Champagne France

trips.name: Ardèche Gorges & the Pont d'Arc
trips.description: The Ardèche river carved Europe's deepest canyon south of the Massif Central. The Pont d'Arc is a natural limestone arch 60m wide where the river emerges from its gorge. The Chauvet Cave's 36,000-year-old paintings are nearby.
trips.cover_search_query: Ardèche gorge Pont d'Arc natural arch France

trips.name: Anjou Wine & Tuffeau Troglodytes
trips.description: Anjou's white wines — Savennières, Quarts de Chaume, Bonnezeaux — are the Loire's finest and least known. The cliff-carved cave towns of Doué-la-Fontaine and Dénezé-sous-Doué add geological wonder.
trips.cover_search_query: Saumur château Loire Valley France

trips.name: Gers & the Armagnac Country
trips.description: The Gers department is Gascony — a land of duck confit, foie gras, Armagnac, and medieval bastide towns where life moves slower than anywhere in France. Auch's cathedral and d'Artagnan's village of Lupiac anchor the route.
trips.cover_search_query: Gers Gascony Armagnac countryside France

trips.name: Vosges du Nord Regional Park
trips.description: Northern Vosges is a UNESCO biosphere reserve of sandstone castles, pine forests, and half-timbered border towns straddling France and Germany. Wissembourg, La Petite Pierre, and the Palatinate Forest extend seamlessly.
trips.cover_search_query: Fleckenstein castle sandstone Vosges France

trips.name: Charente-Maritime — La Rochelle & Ré Island
trips.description: La Rochelle's towers guard a harbour where Huguenots once fled to the Americas. The Île de Ré's cycle paths thread between salt marshes, oyster beds, and whitewashed villages — one of France's finest island escapes.
highlights: Île de Ré cycling and beaches
trips.cover_search_query: Île de Ré salt marshes La Rochelle France

trips.name: Alsace Southern Villages — Thann to Mulhouse
trips.description: The southern Alsace wine route ends at Thann with its gothic collegiate church before opening into the Rhine plain. Mulhouse's remarkable automobile museum is the greatest car collection in Europe.
trips.cover_search_query: Mulhouse Cité Automobile museum Alsace France

trips.name: Southern Burgundy & Golden Stone Villages
trips.description: The golden stone country south of Mâcon — Bresse, Beaujolais, and the Dombes wetlands — is some of the most beautiful countryside in France. Pérouges is the most perfectly preserved 15th-century village in Europe.
trips.cover_search_query: Pérouges medieval village golden stone France

trips.name: Languedoc Coast — Grande-Motte to Sète
trips.description: The Languedoc coast is France's Mediterranean playground — less glamorous than the Riviera but with longer beaches, cheaper wine, and the extraordinary Étang de Thau oyster lagoon at Sète.
trips.cover_search_query: Sète harbour fishing boats Languedoc France

trips.name: Crozon Peninsula & Presqu'île de Crozon
trips.description: The Crozon peninsula is the wildest headland in Brittany — three sub-peninsulas of sheer schist cliffs, secluded beaches, and military heritage from the Pointe des Espagnols to the Pointe de Dinan arch.
trips.cover_search_query: Crozon peninsula cliffs Brittany France

trips.name: Franche-Comté Lakes & the Doubs Valley
trips.description: The Doubs valley carves a meander so tight it almost circles back on itself at the Horseshoe of Pontarlier. The Jura lakes at Clairvaux and Chalain are clean, green, and almost entirely undiscovered by foreign tourists.
trips.cover_search_query: Doubs horseshoe meander Franche-Comté France

trips.name: Manche & the Norman Peninsula
trips.description: The Manche department's oyster beds and bocage hedgerow country are the true heart of rustic Normandy. Coutances cathedral's Romanesque towers rival any in France; the tidal island of Chausey is a hidden gem.
trips.cover_search_query: Coutances cathedral Normandy Manche France

trips.name: Megève & the Fiz Mountains
trips.description: Megève is the most elegant Alpine resort in France — a traffic-free village of cobbled streets and Rococo church below the Aravis peaks. The Combloux balcony viewpoints look straight at Mont Blanc.
trips.cover_search_query: Megève alpine village Mont Blanc France

trips.name: Dentelles de Montmirail & Côtes du Rhône
trips.description: The Dentelles de Montmirail are a jagged limestone lace ridge above the wine villages of Gigondas and Vacqueyras. Mont Ventoux's bald summit dominates the skyline from every direction.
highlights: Mont Ventoux Tour de France climb
trips.cover_search_query: Dentelles de Montmirail Gigondas Provence France

trips.name: Vichy, Riom & the Allier Valley
trips.description: Vichy's Belle Époque thermal halls and Allier riverside parks are surprisingly elegant for a city carrying wartime baggage. Riom's volcanic stone townhouses and Thiers's knife-making heritage complete an off-beat Auvergne circuit.
trips.cover_search_query: Vichy thermal baths Belle Époque Auvergne France

trips.name: Somme & the WWI Battlefields
trips.description: The Somme valley holds the most moving landscape of the Western Front — the Thiepval Arch, Beaumont-Hamel's preserved trenches, and the Vimy Ridge Canadian memorial form a circuit that puts history in brutal perspective.
trips.cover_search_query: Thiepval Arch Somme WWI memorial France

trips.name: Luberon — Marquis de Sade Country
trips.description: The Luberon massif divides Provence into a northern and southern slopes of contrasting character. Lacoste carries the Marquis de Sade's ruined château; Ménerbes was Peter Mayle's 'Year in Provence' village.
trips.cover_search_query: Luberon Bonnieux hilltop village Provence France

trips.name: Northern Alsace — Wissembourg & the Palatinate
trips.description: Northern Alsace is the least touristy part of the region — Wissembourg's half-timbered old town straddles a stream on the German border, while Haguenau's ancient forest and Soultz-sous-Forêts guard Roman remains.
trips.cover_search_query: Wissembourg half-timbered Alsace France

trips.name: Poitou & the Pilgrimage to Santiago
trips.description: Poitiers is where Charles Martel stopped the Moorish advance in 732 AD. The Via Turonensis pilgrimage road from Tours to Santiago passes through Poitiers and Saint-Jean-d'Angély, lined with Romanesque masterpieces.
trips.cover_search_query: Saint-Savin Romanesque frescoes Poitou France

trips.name: Vendée & the Atlantic Marshes
trips.description: The Vendée's 250km of Atlantic coast has France's sunniest and most family-friendly beaches. The bocage interior carries memories of the Vendée Wars; Puy du Fou's historical theme park is Europe's most awarded.
trips.cover_search_query: Noirmoutier island salt marshes Vendée France
